name: Lab Interpretation — Coagulation Panel DIC Pattern
description: Level 3 — handcrafted — DIC pattern recognition requiring multi-parameter correlation
prompt:
Interpret the following laboratory results for a 45-year-old female admitted to ICU with septic shock following complicated obstetric delivery:

Coagulation:
- PT: 28 sec (reference: 9.4–12.5 sec)
- INR: 2.5 (reference: 0.9–1.1)
- PTT: 78 sec (reference: 25–36.5 sec)
- Fibrinogen: 80 mg/dL (reference: 180–400 mg/dL)
- D-dimer: 18.5 mg/L FEU (reference: <0.5 mg/L FEU)
- Thrombin Time: prolonged

Hematology:
- Platelet count: 45,000 /uL (reference: 150,000–400,000)
- Hemoglobin: 7.8 g/dL (reference: 12.0–16.0)
- Blood smear: schistocytes 4+ per high-power field

Chemistry:
- LDH: 1,850 IU/L (reference: 140–280 IU/L)
- Indirect Bilirubin: 3.2 mg/dL (reference: 0–0.8 mg/dL)

Provide clinical interpretation of these results.

abnormal_values.significance: Severe coagulopathy — clotting factor consumption from DIC
Severely prolonged — intrinsic pathway factor consumption
Critically low from fibrinogen consumption in disseminated clotting
Massively elevated from fibrin degradation — pathognomonic of active DIC
Severe thrombocytopenia from platelet consumption in microthrombi
Fragmented red cells = microangiopathic hemolytic anemia (MAHA) — hallmark of DIC/TMA
Markedly elevated from intravascular hemolysis and tissue hypoxia

expected.interpretation: Classic overt DIC (Disseminated Intravascular Coagulation): consumptive coagulopathy with prolonged PT/PTT/INR, critically low fibrinogen (80 mg/dL), massively elevated D-dimer (18.5), severe thrombocytopenia (45K), and MAHA (schistocytes + elevated LDH + indirect bilirubin). Life-threatening — requires urgent FFP, cryoprecipitate, platelet transfusion, and treatment of underlying trigger.
expected.pattern_recognition: DIC triad: (1) Coagulopathy — PT/PTT/INR all prolonged + fibrinogen critically low; (2) Thrombocytopenia — platelets 45K from consumption; (3) MAHA — schistocytes 4+ + LDH 1850 + indirect bilirubin 3.2. D-dimer 37x ULN confirms active fibrinolysis. This is ISTH overt DIC score ≥5.
differential_considerations: Overt DIC from septic shock (primary diagnosis)
Thrombotic microangiopathy (TMA) / HUS (schistocytes + thrombocytopenia + renal involvement)
Liver failure-associated coagulopathy (fibrinogen low, but D-dimer massively elevated favors DIC over isolated hepatic failure)
